source: Walter Gautschi, Gauss-type quadrature rules for rational functions
established_result: Quadrature rules may be designed to integrate rational functions chosen to match important poles, not only polynomials.
overlap_with_present_work: Direct overlap with the motivation for exploiting a nearby pole.
remaining_distinction: Continuum relative minimax over Lorentzian widths, positive variable-moment path, exact mixed selector, validated decision map.
effect_on_claim: Remove any implication that structure-aware pole matching is new.

source: Daan Huybrechs, On the computation of Gaussian quadrature rules for Chebyshev sets of linearly independent functions
established_result: For Chebyshev systems, positive interior-node Gaussian-type rules can integrate 2l functions with l nodes; complete systems support stepwise increases in exactness.
overlap_with_present_work: Positive exactness hierarchies and Gaussian endpoints are already embedded in general moment-space/Chebyshev-system theory.
not_new_in_present_work: The generic idea of a positive exactness hierarchy or a Gaussian endpoint.
remaining_distinction: The continuum Stieltjes minimax objective and the rule-selection problem under a mixed smooth/structural model.
effect_on_claim: Treat the feasible-range and endpoint theorem as structural characterization within the model, not stand-alone novelty.

source: Filip, Nakatsukasa, Trefethen, Beckermann, Rational minimax approximation via adaptive barycentric representations
established_result: Rational minimax approximation, Remez-type algorithms, equioscillation, and uniqueness under standard type/defect conditions are classical and computationally mature.
overlap_with_present_work: The numerical use of equioscillation and minimax optimization.
remaining_distinction: A tailored positive Stieltjes/quadrature subclass with matched moments and a class-specific zero-count certificate.
effect_on_claim: Describe the alternation theorem as a specialization to the admissible class, not a new general minimax principle.

source: Lei-Hong Zhang and Ya-Nan Zhang, b-d-Lawson: a method for the interpolation constrained rational minimax approximation
established_result: Rational minimax approximation under interpolation constraints has an established literature, including derivative constraints, existence/uniqueness questions, and numerical algorithms.
overlap_with_present_work: Matching Laurent coefficients at infinity is related, after z=1/s, to confluent interpolation constraints.
remaining_distinction: Positive-residue Stieltjes realization as a quadrature rule, variable moment depth, continuum Lorentzian objective, selector.
effect_on_claim: Make this the closest approximation-theory comparator and avoid claiming that constraints plus minimax are unprecedented.

source: Andrew Horning and Lloyd N. Trefethen, Quadrature formulas from rational approximations
established_result: Quadrature nodes and weights can be obtained from poles and residues of rational approximations to Cauchy transforms; Gauss quadrature is linked to Padé approximation at infinity.
overlap_with_present_work: Very close conceptual connection between Stieltjes/Cauchy rational approximation and quadrature.
not_new_in_present_work: The broad bridge from rational approximation of a transform to quadrature, or the Padé/Gauss connection.
remaining_distinction: Positivity imposed throughout, variable polynomial-moment prefix, relative minimax over a continuum, exact mixed selector.
effect_on_claim: Cite prominently in the Introduction and Discussion; recenter novelty on the combined model-aware design.

source: van den Bos and Sanderse, A geometric approach for the addition of nodes to an interpolatory quadrature rule with positive weights
established_result: The geometry of preserving positive weights under node addition, removal, and replacement is established; nested positive rules are actively constructed.
overlap_with_present_work: Positivity as a geometric quadrature constraint.
remaining_distinction: Fixed-node-budget minimax trade-off in a Stieltjes response class.
effect_on_claim: Retain positivity as a modeling restriction, not novelty.

source: Jan Glaubitz, Constructing Positive Interpolatory Cubature Formulas
established_result: Positive formulas exact on general finite-dimensional function spaces can be constructed, building on Tchakaloff-type existence.
remaining_distinction: Uniform structural minimax and an explicit risk-based selector.
effect_on_claim: Do not list positivity plus exactness as a primary novelty.

source: Keshavarzzadeh, Kirby, Narayan, Generation of Nested Quadrature Rules for Generic Weight Functions via Numerical Optimization
established_result: Moment-matching quadrature systems can be generated by nonlinear optimization, including nested rules for general weights.
remaining_distinction: Remez/minimax structural objective and exact selector across moment depths.
effect_on_claim: Treat the optimizer as implementation, not core novelty.

source: Beckermann, Bisch, Luce, On the rational approximation of Markov functions
established_result: Relative rational approximation and optimized interpolation for Markov functions are established.
overlap_with_present_work: Same broad Markov/Stieltjes analytic geometry and relative error.
not_new_in_present_work: Relative rational approximation of Markov functions.
remaining_distinction: Positive quadrature realization and mixed-class rule selection.

source: Goda, Kazashi, Tanaka, How sharp are error bounds? Lower bounds on quadrature worst-case errors for analytic functions
established_result: Worst-case error analysis for analytic function classes and comparisons of classical quadratures are established.
remaining_distinction: Exact coefficient-box dual norm combined additively with a Stieltjes structural component to select among constrained rules.
effect_on_claim: Treat the coefficient-box identity as a transparent lemma; emphasize the combined selector and certified partition.

source: Satoshi Hayakawa, Convex-Geometric Error Bounds for Positive-Weight Kernel Quadrature
established_result: Positive-weight worst-case kernel quadrature has modern convex-geometric error theory and constructive algorithms.
remaining_distinction: Deterministic Stieltjes structural family and a finite hierarchy selected by an exact mixed model.
effect_on_claim: Add as current broad context, not a direct competitor.

source: Nailwal and Zalar, Gaussian Quadratures with prescribed nodes via moment theory
established_result: Moment theory continues to characterize constrained/minimal Gaussian quadrature with prescribed nodes.
remaining_distinction: Continuum structural minimax with positive movable nodes/weights and model-aware selection.
effect_on_claim: Keep the dimension theorem as model-specific structure.
